chapters.summary: Council staff calls the hearing to order, welcomes attendees, and gives housekeeping reminders.
chapters.title: Call to order and hearing announcements

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Good morning.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Welcome to the New York City Council hearing for the Committee on Parks and Recreation.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Police lines all sell electronic devices.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Moving forward, no one is to approach the days.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Chair Hankerson, we are ready to begin.

chapters.summary: Chair Ty Hankerson celebrates the historic parks budget baseline, thanking advocates, the former chair, and park workers for their fight.
chapters.title: Chair Hankerson celebrates historic parks budget baseline

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Good morning, everyone.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: My name is Ty Hankerson.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: I serve as the chair on the Committee of Parks and Recreation.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: And before I start with the remarks for today, I just want to say that this is an amazing day for parks with a historic baseline, and that is something to be proud of.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: I want to say to all of the advocates, New Yorkers for Parks, Play for Coalition, to everyone who has lent their voice to this fight over the years, to the former chair, Shekar Krishnan, to our speaker, to our finance chair, thank you so much for fighting for our parks.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: This is what our parks deserve.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: And even to all of the parkies, you know, who may not be watching this because I don't know who watches the council channel, but to all of the parkies, this budget is for you.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: So for everyone that's ever felt like your city has not paid attention or taken you for granted, know that today shows that we honor you and that we are fighting for you.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: All right.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: With that.

chapters.summary: Chair Hankerson introduces the pre-considered bill to co-name 103 thoroughfares and public spaces, highlighting several co-namings in his district.
chapters.title: Chair Hankerson outlines 103 thoroughfare co-naming bill

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Today, I'd like to welcome you all to this hearing of the Committee on Parks and Recreation, where we will vote on two bills and a resolution.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: One of the bills is a pre-considered bill that would ceremonially co-name 103, 103,

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: thoroughfares and public spaces throughout the city.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Co-naming bills are the result of Council's periodic process where we seek to honor individuals and organizations that have made lasting contributions to our city.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Some of these include co-names for active community members, heroic New Yorkers who sacrificed their lives for others, people who committed their lives to public service, or those who greatly enriched the city's cultural landscape.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Of the 103 co-namings of this bill, I am proud to have sponsored Sri Lakshmi, Noreen Munder, Reverend Dr.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Emmanuel Osei-Anchapong, and Mrs.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Kathy Hunt in my district.

chapters.summary: Chair Hankerson introduces Intro 859A, sponsored by Council Member Brewer, which requires DORIS to post biographical information for street and park name changes.
chapters.title: Chair Hankerson outlines Intro 859A on biographical info for co-namings

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: We will also vote on Intro 859A, sponsored by Councilmember Brewer, which would specify the process to be used by the Department of Records and Information Services to comply with existing law

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: that requires the posting of biographical information for the subjects of street and park name changes when no biographical information can be otherwise found in the legislative record.

chapters.summary: Chair Hankerson introduces a resolution honoring the 2026 NBA champion New York Knicks with ceremonial location names like Brunson Way and Towns Square.
chapters.title: Chair Hankerson outlines Knicks championship resolution

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Last but certainly not least, we will vote on a resolution honoring our NBA champions, New York Knicks, sponsored by Deputy Speaker Dr.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Nantasha Williams.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: It calls for various famed New York City locations to be ceremonially named for one year in honor of the 2026 champions.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: The names and locations include Fifth Avenue as Knicks and Five Ave, Broadway as Brunson Way, Victory Boulevard in Staten Island as Captain Clutch Boulevard, Knickerbocker Boulevard,

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Avenue in Brooklyn as Alvarado Avenue, Times Square as Towns Square, Queens Boulevard as Hart Boulevard, the Brooklyn Bridge as Bridges Bridge,

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: the Manhattan Bridge as Ananabe Bridge, the Williamsburg Bridge as Coach Brown Bridge, and the Bronx River as Breen River.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: We could have added some more.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: I'd like to congratulate my colleagues who have proposed these pieces of legislation.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: I urge all of my colleagues on the committee to vote in favor of the legislation before us.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Thank you again and welcome.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: At this time, I'd like to turn it over to Councilmember Brewer and then Councilmember Narcis for their statements.

chapters.summary: Council Member Gale Brewer speaks on Intro 859A, explaining its purpose to update the DORIS digital map with bios for 9/11 first responders, and celebrates the co-naming of Cecile Richards Way.
chapters.title: Council Member Brewer on Intro 859A and Cecile Richards Way

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Thank you very much, Chair, and I am here about 859A.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: This is about the omnibus street co-naming bill.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: It is a long history.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: We have a long history in New York.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: And we need tools to remember those who came before us, whether their name is on a building or a street sign or anywhere.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: These folks shaped our neighborhoods and future generations.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: The council passed my local law 104 requiring the Department of Records and Information Services, known as DORIS, to create an interactive digital map of these co-namings.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Every single person we had names of up to that point is in that digital map.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Yet between 2002 and 2003, more than 400 streets were co-named for first responders killed on 9-11, and the city lacked biographical information to update the map.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I remember going to a lot of funerals at that time.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: And to the credit of Doris, particularly former Commissioner Pauline Toole and her staff, they researched all 400

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: of the individuals lost on that terrible day because they could not until then get their bios.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Into 859A allows Doris to update the map so New Yorkers and visitors can quickly learn the stories behind these heroes without having to search through years of records.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I want to say that to the credit of Doris, they felt that it should go through the council to be official and not just go up on the website.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I want to thank Committee Counsel Chris Sartori, Senior Legislative Policy Analyst Patrick Mulville for their work on this, as well as Local Law 104.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I think we're also ecstatic that Cecile Richards will be Cecile Richards Way on the corner of 83rd Street and Central Park West.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: She was a civil rights, women's rights leader of her generation, head of Planned Parenthood for many, many years, and the daughter of Ann Richards, who was governor of Texas for many years.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I want to thank Deborah Maherans and Carol Portlock for their persistence, and also to thank her husband, Kirk Adams, for his support of this co-naming.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Thank you very much.

chapters.summary: Council Member Mercedes Narcisse honors four individuals from her district being co-named: John Leonard, Fred Wilkins, Audrey Dust, and Gianna.
chapters.title: Council Member Narcisse on four co-namings in District 46

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Thank you to my colleagues for supporting this bill, which let us memorialize four heroes from my district, District 46.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: John Leonard ran the legendary Landy's Pork store on Avenue N for over 60 years, the same store

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: his father opened back in 1958 and never let neighbors go, I mean, without help.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: If you're hungry, he got your back.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: If you need something, he got your back.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Whatever the community we're doing, always involve, always go above and beyond.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: And then we have Fred Wilkins, spent almost 40 years entering the call as a volunteer with Flatlands Volunteer Ambulance Corps, training EMT and saving lives every day.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Those are the people we should honor at all time and give them the space where they spend their lives giving back to the community.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Audrey Dust taught at PS91 for 32 years and never stopped giving after even she retired.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: 50 years volunteering at Downstate Medical Center.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Thousands of hats knit by hands she will do and bring to the children.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Those are the things we can never forget.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Gianna.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Was a young person.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: And even through cancer, she was giving, she was organizing.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: She was making even adults feel better in the community.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: She was present for everything that the young folks were doing, all the folks were doing.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: I'm sure she was the youngest one giving back.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: So we are...

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: We're saying to them, thank you for your service.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: You deserve it.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: And the community that you work so hard for will never forget you.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: So thank you, my colleagues.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Thank you, Chair.

chapters.summary: Chair Hankerson transitions to public testimony and calls Leah Cooper and Sharon Marshall to the table.
chapters.title: Chair Hankerson calls public testimony

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilmember.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: At this time, we're going to turn it over to public testimony.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: We're going to call Leah Cooper and Sharon Marshall to the...

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Not to the stand.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: To the table.

chapters.summary: Sharon Marshall, president-elect of the Brooklyn Alumni Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta, speaks in support of co-naming Union Street as Edith Mott Young Way.
chapters.title: Sharon Marshall testifies for Edith Mott Young Way

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: So good morning.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: I want to thank you, Chair Hankerson and the distinguished members of the Parks and Recreation Committee for giving me this opportunity to speak on behalf of the co-naming of

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: Edith Mott Young Way on Union between Albany and Kingston Avenues in Brooklyn, New York.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: My name is Sharon Marshall.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: I am the president-elect for the Brooklyn Alumni Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Incorporated.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: In fact, my term starts tomorrow, well, tonight at midnight.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: And so I want to share with you that Brooklyn Alumni Chapter has over 350 members that do significant work in the community.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: But to be able to also have it anchored with this co-naming as a place where a founder of Delta Sigma Theta actually resided during...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: Her time is especially poignant and significant for us.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: If you are not familiar, Delta Sigma Theta was founded in 1913 at Howard University by 22 visionary women, including our founding soror, Edith Mott Young.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sharon Marshall
utterances.text: And so I will turn it over to Sor Leah Cooper, who is our chair of Heritage and Archives, who has worked diligently to have this day happen.

chapters.summary: Leah Cooper, Heritage and Archives Chair for Delta Sigma Theta, testifies in support of the Edith Mott Young Way co-naming, detailing Young's legacy as a founder of the sorority and her contributions to Brooklyn.
chapters.title: Leah Cooper testifies for Edith Mott Young Way

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Good morning, Chair Hankerson and distinguished members of the Committee on Parks and Recreation.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Thank you for the opportunity to testify today.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: My name is Leah Cooper and I serve as the Heritage and Archives Chair for Brooklyn Alumni's Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Incorporated.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: I respectfully ask for your favorable vote to co-name Union Street between Albany and Kingston in Brooklyn as Edith Mott Youngway.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Street namings are among the most meaningful ways New York City preserves its history.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: They remind us that the people who shared our neighborhoods deserve to be remembered where they lived, served, and inspired others.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Edith Mott Young was one of the 22 founders of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Incorporated, but today we are not asking you to honor her because she helped found a historic organization.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: We are asking you to honor her because she made Brooklyn her home, devoted her life to education and public service, and in 1949, she helped establish what became the Brooklyn Alumni Chapter.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: From that beginning grew more than 75 years of scholarships, mentoring, youth development, health initiatives, civic engagement, and

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: and community service that continue to benefit Brooklyn today.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: This proposal is supported by a broad coalition of residents, elected officials, community organizations, clergy, businesses, and neighborhood partners because Edith Mott Young,

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Young's legacy belongs not only to Delta Sigma Theta, but to

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Brooklyn itself.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Recognizing her does not rewrite history, it completes it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: It ensures that a woman whose contributions have shaped this, our borough, for generations is finally recognized in this public landscape where her legacy began.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: By voting yes today, you will preserve Brooklyn's history, honoring a lifetime of service, and inspire future generations to ask, who was Edith Mott Young?

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Every time the question is asked, another chapter of our city's history will be told.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: On behalf of the Brooklyn Alumni Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Incorporated, Edith Mott Young's family,

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: and the many lives her legacy continues to touch, I respectfully ask for your favorable vote to establish Edith Mott Young Way.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Leah Cooper
utterances.text: Thank you for your time, your consideration, and your service to the people of New York City.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: At this time, we are going to call the...

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Hello.

chapters.summary: Committee Clerk William Martin announces the roll call vote on all coupled items.
chapters.title: Committee Clerk announces roll call vote

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: William Martin, Committee Clerk, Roll Call Vote Committee on Parks and Recreation.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: All items are coupled.

chapters.summary: Committee members cast their votes, with Council Members Sanchez and Morano explaining their votes to honor local families and Staten Islanders Dorothy Day and John Hudson Dilgen.
chapters.title: Roll call vote on coupled items

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Chair Hankerson.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Would I?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Krishnan.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Lee.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Marte.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Narcisse.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Nurse.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Sanchez.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: The same thing earlier.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: I just want to uplift a few of our local families, Luisa Pinheiro's family and Gloria Thomas'family, who we are also renaming thoroughfares for, and I look forward to those ceremonies with them.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: And with that, I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Ong.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Salaam.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Santos Suoso.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Moran, all.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: I'd like to enthusiastically vote aye, but I really want to thank my colleagues for their votes for the street namings in my district, especially for two incredible Staten Islanders, one woman,

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: both of whom should be saints, one of whom is a household name, and that's Dorothy Day.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: A lot of you may have seen her picture on the wall at City Hall.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: And we're going to be honoring her on her birthday on November 8th.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: And the other is a young man who you might not have heard of named John Hudson Dilgen, who should also be a saint, who passed away far too young after dealing his whole life with a debilitating disease called EB.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: And through his work and his advocacy, they've really furthered the cause of EB research significantly.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: And he's been a model Staten Islander.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: And I thank you all for your support for those street namings in my district.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: With that, I'll vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: The committee clerk announces the final vote of 11 in the affirmative, zero in the negative, approving all items.
chapters.title: Committee Clerk announces 11-0 vote, all items approved

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: I have a vote of 11 in the affirmative, zero in the negative, and no abstentions.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: All items have been approved by the committee.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Mr.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Chair, that is a full committee.

chapters.summary: Chair Hankerson adjourns the committee meeting.
chapters.title: Chair Hankerson adjourns meeting

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Thank you, Mr.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Clerk.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: This committee is adjourned.

summary: The Committee on Parks and Recreation votes on Intro 859A, which requires the online publication of biographical information for street and park co-namings, and a resolution honoring the 2026 NBA champion New York Knicks.
The committee also votes on a pre-considered bill to ceremonially co-name 103 thoroughfares and public spaces across the city.
Public testimony is heard from members of the Delta Sigma Theta Sorority in support of the Edith Mott Young Way co-naming in Brooklyn.
Council Members Gale Brewer and Mercedes Narcisse provide remarks honoring individuals being co-named in their districts, including Cecile Richards and several local community leaders.
title: Committee on Parks and Recreation
